title: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
introduction: This lesson explores how electric current generates magnetic fields and the resulting interactions between magnets and electric current-carrying conductors.
visual_description: Imagine a straight wire carrying an electric current, visualize a circular magnetic field around it.

sections.heading: Right-hand Rule
sections.content: Learn the right-hand rule to determine the direction of magnetic fields and magnetic moments.

sections.heading: Motor Effect
sections.content: Understand how a current-carrying conductor experiences a force when placed in a magnetic field, leading to the rotation of a coil (simple motor).

activity: Conduct an experiment to create a simple motor by winding a copper wire around a nail and connecting it to a battery.
summary: Understanding the magnetic effects of electric current is crucial for various practical applications such as motors and generators.
key_facts: The right-hand rule helps determine the direction of magnetic fields and magnetic moments.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force when placed in a magnetic field, leading to the rotation of a coil (motor effect).
Electric motors work based on the motor effect.
